title: chrysopsis scabrella
intro: Chrysopsis scabrella, also known as the golden aster or goldenray, is a perennial plant native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Chrysopsis scabrella include its ability to soothe and calm irritated skin, reduce pain and inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of Chrysopsis scabrella can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil, and it is often used in salves and topical creams to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
